hero.title: English Teaching Section of the Association of Pedagogues of Cuba
hero.subtitle: APC-ELI provides professional development opportunities for English teachers in Cuba, keeping them in touch with relevant topics and events through newsletters, seminars and other events.

aboutPreview.title: About APC-ELI
aboutPreview.subtitle: The English language specialists section of the Association of Pedagogues of Cuba
aboutPreview.description: APC-ELI (Asociación de Pedagogos de Cuba-Especialistas en Lengua Inglesa) is the English teachers section belonging to the Association of Pedagogues of Cuba. It brings together educators, researchers, and language professionals from across Cuba to advance the quality of English language teaching and learning through conferences, workshops, research grants, and community outreach.

testimonials.title: International Experts Who Have Accompanied Us
testimonials.subtitle: APC-ELI has counted on the collaboration of outstanding international professionals

items.name: Ken Wilson
items.text: Ken Wilson has written over thirty ELT titles, including a dozen textbook series. He was the keynote speaker at the National ELT Colloquium on 'Reducing fear, increasing confidence'.

items.name: Jeremy Harmer
items.role: Author of 'The Practice of English Language Teaching'
items.text: He has taught English in Mexico and the UK, and has trained teachers around the world. His book is a fundamental reference in English teacher training.

items.name: Hugh Dellar
items.text: Teacher and trainer with over twenty years of experience. Co-author of the methodology book 'Teaching Lexically'.

items.name: Susan Piper
items.text: She has actively participated in several APC-ELI webinars, leading sessions on Reading both at the National ELT Colloquium and the Master's program at Varona University.

about.title: About APC-ELI
about.subtitle: Our mission, vision, and commitment to English language education in Cuba

history.title: Our History
history.content: APC-ELI (Asociación de Pedagogos de Cuba-Especialistas en Lengua Inglesa) is the English teachers section belonging to the Association of Pedagogues of Cuba. Since its founding, it has worked to bring together English educators from across the country, fostering professional development, international collaboration, and excellence in language teaching. APC-ELI has actively participated in international events such as IATEFL (Liverpool 2019), association meetings in Lima, Peru, and has collaborated closely with the British Council in organizing British Week, winter courses, and on-site workshops.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: To advance the quality of English language education in Cuba through professional development, research, collaboration, and advocacy, empowering educators to create meaningful learning experiences for their students.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.content: A Cuba where every student has access to high-quality English language education taught by well-prepared, supported, and inspired educators, fostering greater international understanding and opportunity.

objectives.title: Our Objectives
items: Promote innovative teaching methodologies in English language classrooms across Cuba
Facilitate professional development opportunities for English language educators at all levels
Support and disseminate research in English language teaching and learning
Foster international collaboration and cultural exchange programs
Encourage participation of young teachers and undergraduate students in conferences and events
Build a vibrant community of practice among English language professionals

items.title: Excellence
items.description: We strive for the highest standards in English language teaching and professional development.

items.title: Collaboration
items.description: We believe in the power of working together across institutions, borders, and disciplines.

items.title: Innovation
items.description: We embrace new ideas, methodologies, and technologies that enhance language education.

items.title: Inclusion
items.description: We are committed to equitable access to quality English language education for all Cubans.

items.title: Integrity
items.description: We operate with transparency, professionalism, and respect for all members of our community.

items.title: Impact
items.description: We measure our success by the positive change we create in classrooms and communities.

why.title: Why APC-ELI Matters
why.content: In an era of global connection, English proficiency opens doors to educational opportunities, professional advancement, and cultural exchange. APC-ELI plays a crucial role in supporting the educators who make this possible. As a specialized section of the Association of Pedagogues of Cuba, we work to provide professional opportunities to members from across the country.

board.title: Board of Directors
board.subtitle: Meet the leadership team of APC-ELI

members.name: DrC. Eduardo Garbey Savigne
members.bio: National Coordinator of APC-ELI. Represented the association at IATEFL 2019 in Liverpool, funded by the British Council, the UK teachers' union, and individual contribution from Russell Stannard.

members.name: DrC. Isora Enríquez
members.bio: Executive member of APC-ELI. Represented the association at the associations meeting in Lima, Peru alongside Professor Ildefonso Díaz Sandoval from Artemisa province.

members.name: DrC. Liliana Casar
members.bio: Executive member of APC-ELI. Participated in IATEFL 2019 in Liverpool representing the association, funded by the British Council.

members.name: Lisset Rubio
members.bio: Executive member of APC-ELI, contributing to the development and management of the section's activities.

members.name: María de los A. Reyes
members.bio: Executive member of APC-ELI, actively participating in the planning and execution of the association's initiatives.

members.name: Heriberto R. García
members.bio: Executive member of APC-ELI, collaborating on the activities and projects of the English language specialists section.

conference.title: National Conference
conference.subtitle: 5th APC-ELI National Conference
conference.description: The APC-ELI National Conference is the section's main event, bringing together English teachers from across the country. The 5th edition was held on December 10-11, 2020 in Havana, featuring presentations, interactive workshops, research papers, and networking opportunities. The event encourages participation from both presenters and non-presenters, with an emphasis on young teachers and undergraduate students.

participants.title: Participation
participants.description: Teachers from across the country, researchers, and undergraduate students attend the conference. Research papers, pedagogical experiences, and practical workshops on English language teaching are presented.
participants.audience: English teachers and professors, researchers, undergraduate students

grants.title: Grants & Workshops
grants.subtitle: Professional development and funding opportunities for English language educators

grantsSection.title: Grants & International Opportunities
grantsSection.description: APC-ELI manages international scholarship opportunities for its members, including IATEFL scholarships that allow Cuban educators to participate in international events. We believe in providing professional opportunities to members from all over the country.

workshopsSection.title: Workshops
workshopsSection.description: Our professional development workshops are designed to enhance teaching skills, introduce new methodologies, and foster collaboration among English language educators across Cuba.

newsletters.title: Newsletters
newsletters.subtitle: Stay informed with the latest news, research, and updates from APC-ELI
newsletters.description: Our quarterly newsletter keeps members informed about upcoming events, recent research, professional development opportunities, and organizational news. Browse our archive below.

items.title: APC-ELI Newsletter
items.summary: Summary of the executive's semi-annual meeting, agreements, and upcoming activities. Participation in IATEFL Liverpool 2019.

gallery.title: Photo Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Moments from our conferences, workshops, and community events

activities.title: Collateral Activities
activities.subtitle: Webinars, courses, festivals, and community activities
activities.description: APC-ELI develops a wide range of collateral activities including webinars, webcourses, contests, and festivals for in-service teachers, trainees, and students. The pandemic transformed the way teachers delivered classes and communicated with students, boosting online teaching, distance education, and blended learning.

items.title: Webinars with International Experts
items.description: APC-ELI organizes webinars with renowned international experts such as Ken Wilson (author of over 30 ELT titles), Jeremy Harmer (author of The Practice of English Language Teaching), Hugh Dellar (co-founder of Lexical Lab), and Carol Harvey (English communication coach).

items.title: Winter Courses
items.description: Intensive on-site professional development courses at APC headquarters, featuring international workshop leaders such as Julia Simpks (Scotland), who led a 2-day workshop on storytelling.

items.title: English Language Festival
items.description: Annual English language festival with intercultural activities, including sessions on topics such as Scotland and Cuba led by experts like Joan Cutting, retired university professor from Scotland.

items.title: Social and Community Activities
items.description: Young teachers and students actively participate in social and community activities to help sick and vulnerable people, demonstrating APC-ELI's social commitment.

items.title: Hands Up Project
items.description: In collaboration with Susan Piper, leader of the Hands Up Project, reading sessions were developed for students of the Master's program at Universidad Pedagógica Varona, along with webinars on Reading at the National ELT Colloquium.

collaboration.title: Collaborations
collaboration.subtitle: Internal agencies, external agencies, and international support team
collaboration.description: APC-ELI collaborates with various national and international agencies to strengthen English language teaching in Cuba.
